pz-invitation.title: The Restless Invitation
pz-invitation.prompt: Letters moved when the invitation changed. They settle into a hidden instruction.
pz-invitation.inst: Type the hidden eleven-letter instruction. Spaces do not matter.
hints: It names an airport location.
It begins with T.
Two words: eight letters, then three.
TERMINAL TWO.
pz-invitation.why: The shifted letters spell TERMINAL TWO — where the replacement route begins.

pz-packing.title: One Suitcase, Two Nomads
pz-packing.prompt: Pack without crushing the wedding gift or burying the passports.
pz-packing.inst: Enter the item initials in packing order: Clothes, Scarf box, Kippah case, Shoes, Gift, Food, Chargers, Camera, Toiletries, Passports.
hints: Soft clothes form the base.
The scarf box lies flat on the clothes.
Passports go last, on top, reachable.
C S K S G F C C T P.
pz-packing.why: Fragile things ride on soft layers and the passports stay on top.

pz-timetable.title: The Flight That Still Exists
pz-timetable.prompt: Only one flight leaves enough time for the Muscat connection.
hints: The connecting gate closes at 14:05.
A same-terminal transfer needs 50 minutes.
A is delayed; C loses 70 minutes changing terminals.
Choose B.
pz-timetable.why: Flight B lands with a comfortable same-terminal transfer.

pz-scale.title: The Broken Baggage Scale
pz-scale.prompt: Use exactly three reference weights to balance the 22 kg suitcase.
pz-scale.inst: Type the three weights from largest to smallest, separated by commas. Available: 10, 7, 5, 4, 2.
hints: Begin with the 10 kg weight.
Add the 7 kg weight next.
Five kilograms remain.

pz-clocks.title: Four Cities, Four Clocks
pz-clocks.prompt: Set every clock to the same instant as 09:00 in Paris.
pz-clocks.inst: Enter the Istanbul, Muscat and Mumbai times, separated by commas (24-hour).
hints: Istanbul is two hours ahead of Paris.
Muscat is three hours ahead.
Mumbai is four and a half hours ahead.
pz-clocks.why: All four clocks now show one shared instant — except the fifth, stuck on a Havdalah hour.

pz-tiles.title: The Gate in the Tiles
pz-tiles.prompt: Leah feels the mechanical seams: four symbols must be pressed in order.
pz-tiles.inst: Tap the symbols in the order: protector, messenger, direction, destination.
hints: Begin with the protective symbol.
The messenger follows the protector.
The direction comes before the destination.
Hamsa → Rabbit → Arrow → Gate.
pz-tiles.why: The completed pattern slides the tile wall open onto the hidden concourse.

pz-suitcase.title: The Vanishing Suitcase
pz-suitcase.prompt: Who accidentally took the green Mumbai suitcase?
pz-suitcase.inst: Use the destination sticker, the metallic sound and the wedding clue.
options: Caterer · spice jars · flying to Ankara
Architect · rolled plans · flying to Doha
hints: The suitcase carries a Mumbai sticker.
A witness heard metal clinking inside.
One suspect is attending Sharon's wedding.
The musician.
pz-suitcase.why: Only the musician matches destination, sound and occasion.

pz-stars.title: Muscat Under the Stars
pz-stars.prompt: Moshé reads the route diagram; Leah turns the star ceiling. Neither works alone.
pz-stars.inst: Tap the constellations in the diagram's order: East, Crown, Heart, West, South.
hints: Begin at the right-hand edge — the East.
The Crown sits highest of all.
The Heart is the central star.
East → Crown → Heart → West → South.
pz-stars.why: The five stars trace a Hamsa across the ceiling and the gate display flickers back on.

pz-seats.title: The Seating Problem
pz-seats.prompt: Seven passengers, seven seats, and the gate is closing.
pz-seats.inst: Enter the initials for seats A–G in order: Moshé, Leah, Sara, Child, Eli, Flowers, Musician.
hints: The family of three takes C, D and E.
Moshé and Leah stay together in A and B.
The flowers get their own seat F; the musician takes G.
M L S C E F M.
pz-seats.why: Everyone boards: couple together, family together, flowers unsquashed.

pz-corridors.title: Two Corridors, One Arrival
pz-corridors.prompt: Leah sees the power controls; Moshé sees the doors. Each control opens the other's path.
pz-corridors.inst: Tap the six controls in the only safe order.
hints: Nothing opens before Leah restores power.
Moshé opens the gate, then redirects the walkway.
The belt must move before the sign updates.
Power → Gate → Walkway → Belt → Sign → Elevator.
pz-corridors.why: Each traveler opened the other's path until both reached arrivals — together.

pz-havdalah.title: The Havdalah Order
pz-havdalah.prompt: Set the Havdalah items in the order they are used.
pz-havdalah.inst: Tap in order: wine, spices, flame, then the closing blessing.
hints: It begins over a full cup.
The fragrance comes second.
Light before the final words.
Wine → Spices → Flame → Blessing.
pz-havdalah.why: The ceremony completes — and in the candlelight, a white rabbit sits calmly on the table.

pz-future-object.title: The Object from Later
pz-future-object.prompt: One object on the debris hill does not belong to this afternoon.
pz-future-object.inst: Choose the object that comes from the future of this memory.
options: A metro card, dated years from now
A broken brick with old stamps
hints: Three objects have aged here for years.
One object hasn't been printed yet.
Moshé talked about the metro on the rooftop — later.
The metro card.
pz-future-object.why: A future metro card inside an old memory: proof the past is being reconstructed, not replayed.

pz-footprints.title: Footprints That Never Left
pz-footprints.prompt: Read the beach: whose prints are whose, and what is impossible about them?
pz-footprints.inst: Choose the statement the sand actually proves.
options: Every trail arrives; not one trail leaves
A dog chased the gulls at dawn
The tide erased half the story
hints: Count trails toward the water.
Now count trails coming back.
Zero is a strange number of departures.
Every trail arrives; none leave.
pz-footprints.why: The direction anomaly, stated plainly: in this reconstruction, arrivals are real and departures are not.

pz-skyline.title: Two Skylines
pz-skyline.prompt: Leah sees structures; Moshé sees the city's story. Only overlaid do they match the view.
pz-skyline.inst: Switch perspectives, note which towers each of you can identify, then tap the towers left to right.
hints: The oldest silhouette stands far left.
The crane leans beside it.
The antenna ends the line on the right.
Old Tower → Crane → New Tower → Antenna.
pz-skyline.why: The overlaid skylines agree — except one tower that belongs to a later city.

pz-bridge.title: The Opinionated Bridge
pz-bridge.prompt: Cross with camera, dinner and scooter — the rotten plank takes at most one light load.
pz-bridge.inst: Type the crossing order, comma separated: Moshe, Leah, Scooter, Camera.
hints: The lightest precious thing goes first, carried high.
Leah tests the rail path with the camera.
The scooter rolls across last, alone.
Camera, Leah, Moshé, Scooter.
pz-bridge.why: Load path respected, camera dry, dinner intact, scooter's one brake unharmed.

pz-settlement.title: The Settlement Model
pz-settlement.prompt: Leah places structures; Moshé arranges the plan. The model wants both.
pz-settlement.inst: Tap the pieces in build order: water first, homes before square, square before gate.
hints: No settlement without water on the high point.
Homes come before their meeting place.
The gate is the last promise.
Water Tower → Houses → Square → Gate.
pz-settlement.why: Engineer and planner build one small town — and its sign already says WELCOME HOME.

pz-groupphoto.title: Fourteen of Sixteen
pz-groupphoto.prompt: Sixteen volunteers, fourteen in the photo. Who is missing?
pz-groupphoto.inst: Choose who the photo cannot show.
options: The two cooks — they were serving
hints: The cooks are visible, aprons and all.
Nobody overslept — attendance was taken.
Who was missing from the Mumbai photo?
Leah and Moshé — the same two, every photo.
pz-groupphoto.why: You cannot appear in a memory you are still rebuilding. The pattern holds.

pz-oldcity.title: Four Quarters, One Path
pz-oldcity.prompt: Cross the Old City visiting each quarter once, never crossing your own path.
pz-oldcity.inst: Tap the quarters in a walkable order starting nearest the hostel gate.
hints: Start where the morning market noise is loudest.
Jeff's 'second arch, obviously' comes second — obviously.
Finish above the city, on the rooftops.
Market Lane → Second Arch → Fountain Court → Rooftop Path.
pz-oldcity.why: One clean line through the city — and from the rooftop path, the lane that shouldn't exist is briefly visible.

pz-sixturns.title: Six Wrong Turns
pz-sixturns.prompt: Recount the six wrong turns of that Shabbat walk. Drawn on paper, they make a letter.
pz-sixturns.inst: Type the six turns in order as L or R, no spaces. They began by turning left at the bakery.
hints: It starts with L.
Two rights follow the first left.
It ends turning right, under the lamp.
L R R L L R.
pz-sixturns.why: Traced on the map, the turns draw a letter S — the first stroke of a sentence still being written.

pz-signs.title: The Sentence in the Signs
pz-signs.prompt: Moshé reads the street signs in walking order. They form a question.
pz-signs.inst: The signs read: RUE DU REPOS, AVENUE DES VOYAGEURS, PLACE DE LA QUESTION. Type the one French word the three share a theme with: the word for 'stay' — RESTER — appears hidden in one sign. Type that sign's key word.
hints: Look at the first sign.
'Repos' means rest — rest and staying share a root of stillness.
The other two signs are travelers and a question.
REPOS.
pz-signs.why: Rest, travelers, a question: the street grid itself is asking whether to keep moving or to stay.

pz-battery.title: One Battery, Two Bikes
pz-battery.prompt: 40% battery, two bikes, 8 km of gravel. Assist drains 10% per km but only one bike can use it at a time.
pz-battery.inst: Swapping the battery costs nothing. What is the greatest assisted distance, in km, you can share before it dies?
hints: 10% per assisted kilometre.
It doesn't matter who uses it — the pool is shared.
4 kilometres of assist, shared however you like.
pz-battery.why: Four assisted kilometres, alternated fairly — and four pedaled ones, complained about equally.

pz-timeline.title: The Shuffled Shabbat
pz-timeline.prompt: The border day refuses to stay in order. Rebuild it.
pz-timeline.inst: Tap the moments in true order.
pz-timeline.answer: Morning walk|Bridge crossing|Late lunch|Reading on the grass
hints: The day began on foot, early.
You crossed the bridge with morning light behind you.
Lunch was late; the grass came after.
Walk → Bridge → Lunch → Grass.
pz-timeline.why: The day reassembles: quiet, bordered by two countries, ordered by light.

pz-ticket-route.title: The Pouch of Old Tickets
pz-ticket-route.prompt: Reconstruct the Riviera hops from the ticket stubs — and notice what every stub shares.
pz-ticket-route.inst: Type the route as city initials in travel order: Antibes, Menton, Ventimiglia, and back to Antibes.
hints: It starts where the fishing rods are.
The Citron Festival is the second stop.
Italy is the farthest point, then home.
A M V A.
pz-ticket-route.why: Every 'wrong' connection on the route still shows two adjacent seats. The itinerary optimized for togetherness.

pz-blessings.title: Six of Seven
pz-blessings.prompt: The booklet holds six blessings. Which of the seven is missing?
options: The blessing of joy and gladness
The blessing of building a home together
The blessing of the voices of the couple
hints: Wine opens the booklet — it's there.
Joy and voices both appear on the last page.
Which blessing is about what two people build?
The blessing of building a home together.
pz-blessings.why: The page about building a home is the one the memory cannot supply — the players must supply it themselves.

pz-mirror.title: The Stone Doubles
pz-mirror.prompt: Leah maps the niches; Moshé matches the faces. Cross-check both lists.
pz-mirror.inst: How many visitors in the cave have no stone double? Type the number.
hints: Moshé matched every face he could.
Leah counted two empty niches.
The empty niches are exactly where two doubles should stand.
pz-mirror.why: Two visitors have no doubles: the same two missing from every photograph.

pz-train.title: The Arguing Timetable
pz-train.prompt: Board says platform 5, sign says 3, conductor says yes. The Udaipur train leaves in 9 minutes.
pz-train.inst: Choose the evidence that actually identifies the right platform.
options: Follow the wedding musician's metal luggage
hints: The board and sign contradict — one of them updated late.
Crowds follow the board, including its mistakes.
Someone you've met before is also going your way.
The musician — Mumbai wedding, Udaipur honeymoon tour. Follow the clinking.
pz-train.why: The musician's luggage has been right twice already. Platform 3, as the sign said.

pz-cablecar.title: The Cable Car Limit
pz-cablecar.prompt: The car takes 200 kg per trip. Loads: 90, 80, 70, 60, 50. Everyone rides before the fireworks — in three trips.
pz-cablecar.inst: Type the three trips as weight pairs, heaviest trip first, e.g. 90+80,70+60,50
hints: 90 and 80 together stay under 200.
70 and 60 pair cleanly.
The lightest rides alone with the view.
pz-cablecar.why: Three trips, no group split at the top, and the fireworks catch everyone mid-air on the last one.

pz-amber.title: The Wall's Rhythm
pz-amber.prompt: The Amber wall repeats every five merlons, then breaks. Breaks mark the path.
pz-amber.inst: Tap the wall sections in path order.
hints: Follow the breaks in the order the wall climbs.
Lowest break first.
The arch is the destination, not a break.
First → Second → Third → Gate arch.
pz-amber.why: The breaks stitch a walkable line up the hill, straight to the gate before sunset.

pz-fuzzy.title: The Case of Fuzzy's Tail
pz-fuzzy.prompt: Fuzzy the costume-dog has lost his tail during Purim-meets-Holi. Everyone is pink.
pz-fuzzy.inst: Choose the culprit using the three facts: the tail's hook was unclipped, not torn; the culprit is shorter than the hook; green powder was found on the clip.
options: The tall dancer covered in pink
The small kid whose powder bag was green
The Holi wind, blamed for everything
hints: Unclipped means hands, not accident.
The hook sits low — or the culprit reached up? No: shorter than the hook.
Who carried green?
The small kid with the green powder bag. The tail is now a Holi trophy.
pz-fuzzy.why: Unclipped by small hands dusted green. The tail was returned, ceremonially, at sundown.

pz-bargain.title: The Fair Meter
pz-bargain.prompt: Quoted 300. The meter says 8 per km, the map says 12 km, and dignity is non-negotiable.
pz-bargain.inst: Type the fair price Moshé negotiates to, in rupees. He offers meter price plus a 24-rupee tip for the shrine's new rabbit.
hints: Meter: 8 × 12.
That's 96.
Plus 24 for the rabbit figurine's welfare.
pz-bargain.why: Both sides declare victory, which is the definition of a fair price. The rabbit figurine nods.

pz-symmetry.title: The Flaw in the Reflection
pz-symmetry.prompt: Building and reflection differ in exactly one detail.
pz-symmetry.inst: Choose the detail the reflection adds.
options: A bird that isn't in the sky
hints: Count the big shapes — they all match.
It's small, warm and yellow.
Someone seems to be home in the reflection only.
One extra lit window.
pz-symmetry.why: The memory added a lit window: somewhere in the reconstruction, a home is already waiting.

pz-campsite.title: Camp Before Dark
pz-campsite.prompt: Two workers, four jobs, one correct interleaving before the light goes.
pz-campsite.inst: Tap the jobs in order: his windbreak, her anchors, his fire, her guylines.
hints: Wind first, or nothing else survives.
The tent must be anchored before the fire is lit beside it.
Guylines are the final tension.
Windbreak → Anchors → Fire → Guylines.
pz-campsite.why: Camp stands, fire burns, stars come out early — and a letter fragment waits in the tent pocket.

pz-canal.title: Four Stops Before Closing
pz-canal.prompt: Chabad, Chinatown, the floating market and the pier — one boat line, one direction, market closes first.
pz-canal.inst: Tap the stops in the only order that catches everything.
hints: Start where breakfast (and the shakshuka pizza) is.
The market closes at noon — go early.
Neon is better after dark: Chinatown late.
Chabad → Floating market → Chinatown → Pier.
pz-canal.why: Every stop caught in its best hour, and the loop-canal with no exit safely avoided.

pz-tides.title: The Sea's Timetable
pz-tides.prompt: The lagoon needs mid-tide; the cave mouth needs anything below high.
pz-tides.inst: Choose the departure that sees both.
hints: Dead low strands the lagoon boat.
High tide seals the cave.
One entry on the chart is an anomaly, not an option.
09:30, rising mid-tide.
pz-tides.why: Mid-tide threads both needles. The impossible 16:30 low is logged in the notebook instead.

pz-coconut.title: The Load-Bearing Coconuts
pz-coconut.prompt: Seven coconuts, three smoothie orders: one double, two singles. A double takes two coconuts... and Moshé needs two just to hold.
pz-coconut.inst: How many coconuts remain for the courage reserve after the orders are filled? Type the number.
hints: The double order: 2 coconuts.
Two singles: 2 more. Total 4 for drinks.
Moshé is holding 2 for courage.
pz-coconut.why: One coconut remains — and during a certain hypothetical question, it rolled away, unneeded.

pz-visual-route.title: Navigation by Pictures
pz-visual-route.prompt: No shared language. The guesthouse card shows: fish, umbrella, golden bowl, moon.
pz-visual-route.inst: Tap the signs in the card's order.
hints: The card reads top to bottom.
Fish first — the harbor end of the street.
The moon marks the guesthouse door.
Fish → Umbrella → Golden bowl → Moon.
pz-visual-route.why: Pictures outlast words: four signs later, home. (The fish-umbrella-bowl shop dinner happened anyway.)

pz-seder.title: Seder by the Sea
pz-seder.prompt: The outdoor seder must begin in order — before the water festival reaches the table.
hints: It begins with sanctifying over the cup.
Washing comes second.
The greens before the breaking.
Kiddush → Urchatz → Karpas → Yachatz.
pz-seder.why: The seder begins correctly; the matzah under the hat survives Songkran entirely.

pz-elephant.title: The Elephant's Round
pz-elephant.prompt: The elephant visited: pagoda, bridge, pagoda, gate, pagoda, bridge, pagoda, ... The bananas move in a pattern.
hints: Write the sequence down: P B P G P B P …
Pagoda alternates with something every time.
The something cycles: bridge, gate, bridge, …
The gate.
pz-elephant.why: Pattern PBPGPBP(G): pagoda every other stop, the alternates cycling bridge-gate. The bananas confirm.

pz-whitetemple.title: Road of Frost and Haze
pz-whitetemple.prompt: High road closes at noon. Hot spring adds an hour. Temple face lights at four.
pz-whitetemple.inst: Tap the day into its only working order.
hints: Coffee first — it's on the way and opens early.
Beat the fire-haze closure: high road before noon.
The spring fills the afternoon gap.
Café → High road → Hot spring → Temple at four.
pz-whitetemple.why: The temple catches the four o'clock light like frost catching fire.

pz-flags.title: The Broken Run
pz-flags.prompt: Flags run: three red, one banner, repeat. One balcony breaks the run.
pz-flags.inst: The run along the boulevard reads R R R B R R R B R R B R R R B — which position breaks the pattern? Type the position number.
hints: Group in fours: RRRB.
First two groups are perfect.
The third group is a flag short.
Position 11 — a banner arrives one flag early.
pz-flags.why: Under the early banner: the side street, the quiet gap, and the best view of the fireworks.

pz-coaster.title: Rows of Two
pz-coaster.prompt: Six riders, three rows. Birthday girl picks front. The tall friend can't ride behind the hat. The couple won't split.
pz-coaster.inst: Type the rows front to back as initial pairs: use B+M (birthday+mother), T+F (tall+friend), L+M2 (the couple). Format: XX,XX,XX with the pair letters only, e.g. BM,TF,LM
hints: Front row is fixed: BM.
The mother's hat is in row one — the tall friend can't sit in row two.
So the couple takes the middle.
BM, LM, TF.
pz-coaster.why: Everyone rides, the hat survives until the loop, and the photo at the drop is legendary.

pz-lanterns.title: Lanterns and Reflections
pz-lanterns.prompt: Leah reads the river reflections; Moshé reads the lanterns. Light them so both patterns agree.
pz-lanterns.inst: Tap the lanterns in the order their reflections brighten.
hints: The river brightens warm colors first.
Red, then gold.
White last — it's the unlit one's twin.
Red → Gold → Blue → White.
pz-lanterns.why: When the white lantern lights, its reflection finally matches — and the rabbit tally in the notebook aligns into a pattern.

pz-basket.title: The Spinning Basket
pz-basket.prompt: The basket boat spins unless the weight sits opposite and low.
options: Both sit on the same side, holding the rim
One stands to paddle, one sits center
Both sit low, opposite each other, paddle strokes alternating
Spin on purpose and call it a ride
hints: Same side capsizes — memorably.
Standing raises the center of gravity.
Opposite and low, strokes alternating.
C. (D was attempted. Once.)
pz-basket.why: Opposite, low, alternating: the basket goes forward — upstream, oddly, but forward.

pz-gates.title: Gate Within Gate
pz-gates.prompt: Outer gate: east door for travelers. Middle gate: scholars pass south. Inner gate: the hinge turns one way.
hints: You are travelers first.
Then scholars — Moshé insists.
The hinge admits you once.
East → South → Hinge → Courtyard.
pz-gates.why: Gate within gate within gate — and behind you, each door quietly ceases to exist.

pz-boat.title: Three Cave Mouths
pz-boat.prompt: Left cave: under a meter clearance. Right cave: dry ledge, no water. Middle cave: sings.
options: The left cave — duck and hope
The middle cave — the one that sings
The right cave — walk the boat
Wait for the water to drop
hints: The boat is taller than a meter with you in it.
You can't carry a boat up a dry ledge.
The rower has done this nine thousand times, and she's aiming middle.
The singing middle cave.
pz-boat.why: The middle cave sings because it breathes — open at both ends. The song is Sharon's tune.

pz-terraces.title: The Terrace Rhythm
pz-terraces.prompt: The terraces descend 4, 4, 4, 3, 4, 4, 4, 3 … until one group breaks the rhythm.
pz-terraces.inst: The slope reads 4,4,4,3,4,4,4,3,4,4,3 — which group number breaks the rhythm? Type it.
hints: The pattern repeats in fours: 4,4,4,3.
Two full cycles pass cleanly — that's eight groups.
Count on: 4 (9th), 4 (10th), then…
The 11th group — a 3 arrives early. The hidden path crosses there.
pz-terraces.why: Where the rhythm breaks, a farmer's path crosses the terraces — invisible in the mist, obvious in the count.

pz-mud.title: The Extraction
pz-mud.prompt: Bike in the mud to the axle. Two people, four moves, one order that works.
pz-mud.answer: Boards under wheel|Count to three|Pull the front|Rock the throttle
hints: Traction before force.
Coordination before effort — count first.
Pull and throttle land on the same beat.
Boards → Count → Pull → Throttle.
pz-mud.why: The bike surfaces with a sound like a cork. Both of them are 40% mud. The waterfall applauds.

pz-echo.title: The Echo Chamber
pz-echo.prompt: The cave returns each word altered toward its truth. You called GO. What did the cave answer?
pz-echo.inst: Type the cave's answer. (If sound is off: the wall's mineral streaks spell the same word.)
hints: It is one word, four letters.
It is the opposite of what was called.
The cave has been saying it the whole journey.
STAY.
pz-echo.why: Every departure in this reconstruction is answered the same way. The cave just says it out loud.

pz-neon.title: Signs and Their Twins
pz-neon.prompt: Six reflections in the canal, five signs above. Pair what pairs, and name the orphan.
pz-neon.inst: Choose the reflection with no sign.
hints: The crab is famously real.
The drummer drums above the third bridge.
What keeps appearing where it shouldn't?
The rabbit — a reflection with no sign.
pz-neon.why: Five signs, six reflections. The rabbit shines up from the water with nothing above to cast it.

pz-queue.title: The Queue Theorem
pz-queue.prompt: Left lane: merges every 10 min. Right lane: slow but never stops. Express gate: opens on the quarter hour, from the right lane only.
pz-queue.inst: It is 12:03. Choose the fastest plan.
options: Left lane and pray for merges
Right lane, then express gate at 12:15
hints: The express gate only serves the right lane.
12:15 is twelve minutes away — the right lane survives that.
Splitting up defeats the entire premise of this journey.
C.
pz-queue.why: A scheduling problem wearing a fun hat, solved. Ride photo: two people mid-laugh — from a ride they hadn't boarded yet.

pz-toriipath.title: The Hamsa at the Gates
pz-toriipath.prompt: Hold the Hamsa to each gate. Leah reads the eye; Moshé reads the inscriptions. True gates keep the eye clear.
pz-toriipath.inst: Tap only the true gates, in walking order. The eye clouded at the 2nd and 5th.
hints: Walk in numeric order, skipping the clouded ones.
Skip the second.
Skip the fifth.
First → Third → Fourth → Sixth.
pz-toriipath.why: The Hamsa is a compass for memories: clear eye, true detail; clouded eye, invention.

pz-deer.title: The Map Thief of Nara
pz-deer.prompt: Three deer came within reach: one bowed twice (both hands visible — no maps), one has paper crumbs, one arrived after the bag was already light.
hints: The bower's alibi is airtight and adorable.
The latecomer arrived post-theft.
Paper crumbs are not a natural part of the deer diet. And yet.
The one with the crumbs.
pz-deer.why: Justice in Nara: the deer bowed in apology. The maps were beyond saving. New maps were purchased.

pz-maplayers.title: Two Maps of One City
pz-maplayers.prompt: Leah's layer: rail load and flow. Moshé's layer: districts and eras. One crossing satisfies both.
pz-maplayers.inst: Tap the candidates and keep only the crossing both maps mark. Order: eliminate hers-only, eliminate his-only, then confirm the shared one.
hints: Eliminate before you confirm.
Her junction has flow but no story.
His square has story but no flow.
Eliminate both, confirm the shared crossing.
pz-maplayers.why: Systems and stories agree on exactly one crossing — the famous one, where everyone meets.

pz-fuji.title: The Day in Photographs
pz-fuji.prompt: Order the day's photos by light and logic — then look at what every gate photo shares.
pz-fuji.inst: Tap the photos in the order they were taken.
hints: The mountain unveils at dawn.
The bikes went around the lake in the morning.
Snacks ended the day — remember the box.
Dawn → Lake ride → Flower arch → Snacks.
pz-fuji.why: Sorted, the truth is visible: in every gate and arch, they are walking IN. This reconstruction has no exits.

pz-palace.title: The Compass Gates
pz-palace.prompt: The palace gates open by compass: begin where the sun rises, end where it sets, honoring the main gate between.
hints: Begin with the sunrise.
The main gate faces south — it is honored second.
End with the sunset.
East → South → West.
pz-palace.why: The gates open in the sun's own order. The guard ceremony, this once, runs forward.

pz-seoulmate.title: You Are My…
pz-seoulmate.prompt: He has been building the sentence all day. The last word is a city wearing a word inside it.
pz-seoulmate.inst: Type the final word of the sentence.
hints: It is a pun. She knows it is going to be a pun.
The city is the one you are standing in.
The hidden word inside is 'soul'.
SEOULMATE.
pz-seoulmate.why: A word built from a city, so she would hear the word inside it. The last fragment glows.

pz-reconstruct.title: The Page, Rebuilt
pz-reconstruct.prompt: Eight fragments, one erased page. Order them by the journey.
pz-reconstruct.inst: Tap the fragments in journey order.
pz-reconstruct.answer: Fragment I|Fragment II|Fragment III|Fragment IV|Fragment V|Fragment VI|Fragment VII|Fragment VIII
hints: The fragments are numbered in journey order — trust the numerals.
I through IV cover Paris to India.
V through VIII cover Thailand to Korea.
I, II, III, IV, V, VI, VII, VIII.
pz-reconstruct.why: The page exists again. It is a boarding pass for one seat, on a return flight — with the final line still blank.

pz-evidence.title: People Don't Keep Receipts for Jokes
pz-evidence.prompt: Find the object that proves the thrift-shop wedding rehearsal meant something.
options: The kept receipt, dated and folded small
A photo — except no photo was taken
hints: The hat went back on the rack.
Mirrors keep nothing.
What do you keep folded in a wallet for years?
The receipt.
pz-evidence.why: Kept, folded, carried across nine countries. The joke was never entirely a joke.

pz-phiphi2.title: The Beach, From Her Side
pz-phiphi2.prompt: Replay the sunset from Leah's viewpoint. What could she see that he couldn't?
pz-phiphi2.inst: Tap what her side of the beach revealed, in the order she noticed it.
pz-phiphi2.answer: The pier|The departing ferry|The rabbit on the post
hints: First the place, then the event, then the witness.
The pier framed her whole view.
The ferry left mid-question.
Pier → Ferry → Rabbit.
pz-phiphi2.why: The evening airport ferry left while the question hung in the air. He never looked at it. The rabbit sat exactly where he didn't look.

pz-proposals.title: Six Postcards, Two Truths
pz-proposals.prompt: Test each proposal place against the Hamsa's eye. Four cloud it; two keep it clear.
pz-proposals.inst: Choose the pair the eye stays clear on.
hints: Paris was a coffee stain.
Fuji was rice snacks; the waterfall was just perfect, not proposive.
Where was a question actually asked — and where was a word actually built?
Koh Phi Phi and Seoul.
pz-proposals.why: Every candidate held one authentic clue and one lovely lie. Only the too-early question and the Seoulmate sentence survive the Hamsa's eye.

pz-meta-order.title: Finale I — The Order of Things
pz-meta-order.prompt: Lay the whole story straight: the four turning points, in the order they truly happened.
pz-meta-order.inst: Tap the turning points in true order.
tokens: The word built from a city
pz-meta-order.answer: The rabbit at Havdalah|The question asked too early|The ferry that left alone|The word built from a city
hints: It all starts with an excuse to keep talking.
The question came before the ferry left — that's why the ferry left alone.
The city-word is the most recent.
Havdalah → Question → Ferry → Seoulmate.
pz-meta-order.why: The spine of the story, straightened: an excuse, a question, an unboarded departure, a word.

pz-meta-memories.title: Finale II — The True Memories
pz-meta-memories.prompt: Three of these are true and load-bearing. One is an invention the reconstruction slipped in.
options: The receipt folded in the wallet
hints: The Hamsa's eye stayed clear on the receipt.
The echo was witnessed twice.
One of these was always labelled 'hypothetical'.
The horse wedding — imagined, allergic, and lovely, but never real.
pz-meta-memories.why: The imagined horses gallop off, taking the last red herring with them.

pz-final-phrase.title: Finale III — The Last Line
pz-final-phrase.prompt: The boarding pass is whole except its final line. It is not a destination. It is what Moshé said instead of boarding the missing flight — three words.
hints: Three words. He said them instead of leaving.
The cave already told you the first one.
The Seoulmate sentence tells you whose company he chose.
The cave said the first word. Then ask: with whom? Say it to her.
pz-final-phrase.why: The line writes itself in gold. The missing flight departs, forever, without him — exactly as it always did.
